full_text:
- Uttarakhand Heat Surges As 10 Cities Cross 40°C: Relief Likely In Two Days, Rain Across State, Snowfall In Hills
Uttarakhand’s 10 cities cross 40°C, heat intensifies:Rain likely across state after 2 days, snowfall in hills
- Copy link
Temperatures in several parts of Uttarakhand have surged past the 40°C mark, with at least 10 locations across the districts of Nainital, Udham Singh Nagar and Haridwar recording intense heat, according to the Meteorological Department.
Among the worst-affected areas, Kashipur and Bajpur recorded 42°C each, while Khatima, Kichha and Sitarganj saw temperatures climb to 43°C.
In other areas, temperatures reached 41°C in Ramnagar, 39°C in Haldwani, and 42°C each in Rudrapur and Pantnagar. The mercury stood at 38°C in Dehradun, while Haridwar and Roorkee recorded 42°C each.
The department said dry weather conditions are likely to persist across the state through the day, with temperatures expected to rise further during daytime hours.
However, a change in weather is likely after two days, with rainfall expected in most parts of the state. Higher reaches may also witness fresh snowfall during this period.
Weather PHOTOS
Bright sunshine likely in Dehradun, temperature to touch 38°C
Weather conditions in Dehradun are expected to remain largely clear, with bright sunshine prevailing through the day.
Light clouds may appear intermittently, but they are unlikely to provide any significant relief from the heat. The maximum temperature is expected to hover around 38°C, while the minimum may settle near 20°C.
The daytime heat is likely to intensify, creating heatwave-like conditions in parts of the city, even as overall weather remains stable and dry.
Temperature in major cities on April 22 (in degrees Celsius)
How will the weather be for the next three days
April 24- Light to scattered showers accompanied by thunder are likely in the districts of Uttarkashi, Rudraprayag, Chamoli, Bageshwar and Pithoragarh. Snowfall is expected in areas above 4,000 metres. The remaining districts are likely to experience mainly dry weather.
April 25- Light to moderate rain is likely across the same hill districts — Uttarkashi, Rudraprayag, Chamoli, Bageshwar and Pithoragarh — along with snowfall in higher reaches. Very light rain may occur in other hilly areas, while the plains are expected to remain dry.
April 26- Light rainfall is likely at isolated places in the hill districts, with snowfall continuing in areas above 4,000 metres. The plains are expected to witness dry conditions.
